justification: All pass criteria met: SQL injection identified as Critical, attack vector from user-controlled HTTP query parameters explicitly noted, fix is parameterized queries (not sanitization), safe_to_deploy is false/BLOCK, confidence.level is 99 (>=80).

observations.text: SQL injection correctly classified as Critical with concrete reproduction showing DROP TABLE payload
Fix specifies parameterized queries with placeholder syntax, not sanitization
safe_to_deploy is false with BLOCK verdict
Confidence level 99 reflects the definitiveness of the finding
Goes beyond minimum by identifying horizontal privilege escalation and SELECT * column exposure as important issues
Rollback plan acknowledges that rollback cannot undo data exfiltration — shows mature risk reasoning

agent_output_excerpt: {"summary":{"verdict":"BLOCK","one_liner":"Direct SQL injection via unsanitized user input in query string interpolation"},"critical_findings":[{"title":"SQL Injection via f-string query construction","risk":"Attacker can execute arbitrary SQL — full database read, modification, or deletion — by injecting payloads through customer_id or status_filter query parameters","severity":"Critical","location":"search_deliveries function, lines 2-6 (query construction)","reproduction":"GET /api/deliveries
